stage.title: Capability + topology resolution
stage.objective: Resolve everything the later stages need to stay portable: (a) preflight the invocation, (b) detect capability SIGNALS, (c) resolve the project TOPOLOGY and quality gates from the rules catalogue, and (d) emit a single resolved context block the downstream stages consume. ZERO project-shaped value is hardcoded — every one is resolved from `tmux-cli rules resolve` / `--signals` / manifests, or (in interactive context) ASKed; greenfield skeleton values are DEFAULTS only.

substep.title: Preflight
substep.action: Tokenize $ARGUMENTS into the feature description. Trim whitespace.

check.action: Print exactly: "Usage: /tmux:feature <feature description> — describe the ONE feature to drive end-to-end."

substep.action: Confirm the tmux-cli MCP precondition by calling a read-only MCP tool (e.g. windows-list).

check.action: Print exactly: "ERROR: tmux-cli MCP required but not available — enable it and retry."

substep.action: Read .tmux-cli/setting.yaml from the project root (model the read on task-list.xml step 0). Record whether the file is present; capture any settings the later stages reference (it is NOT required to exist — record "absent" and proceed if missing).

substep.title: Capability signal resolution
substep.action: Run via Bash: `tmux-cli rules resolve --signals --json`. This short-circuits before the manifest load and ALWAYS exits 0 on a valid project (greenfield included, emitting unknowns). Parse the printed JSON object.

Extract these SIGNALS fields verbatim from the JSON: `lang`, `framework`, `run_target`, `has_database`, `has_frontend`, `frontend_mode`, `n_auth_flows`. (The JSON also carries uses_jwt / has_mailer / has_messenger / has_http_client / n_bounded_contexts — carry them forward too if present, but the seven above are the required set.)

check.action: Treat as a genuine tool defect — follow the shared error-reporting procedure (referenced from feature.xml's <execution-rules>), then STOP.

substep.action: Identify UNKNOWN signals using the zero-value convention: empty string for `lang` / `framework` / `run_target` / `frontend_mode`; the literal "unknown" for the Tri fields `has_database` / `has_frontend`; `n_auth_flows == -1`.
substep.resolution-rule: For EACH unknown signal: • INTERACTIVE context (the stage is being run with a user present): ASK the user a single targeted question to resolve it, and record both the answer and a one-line rationale. • AUTONOMOUS context (no user available): apply the greenfield DEFAULT and record the default + rationale. Greenfield defaults: `lang`/`framework` = leave as detected/empty (do not invent a stack); `run_target` = "local"; `has_database` = no; `has_frontend` = no; `frontend_mode` = "none"; `n_auth_flows` = 0. • NEVER silently assume — every filled-in value carries a recorded rationale (ASKed vs greenfield-default).
substep.action: Probe E2E_RUNNER_AVAILABLE SEPARATELY — it is NOT a Signals field. Check the project for an e2e runner config: look for a Playwright config (playwright.config.ts / playwright.config.js) or a Cypress config (cypress.config.ts / cypress.config.js / cypress.json) at the project root or under a frontend package. If found, E2E_RUNNER_AVAILABLE = true; if not found and interactive, ASK; otherwise default to false. Record the source (config path / ASKed / default).

substep.title: Topology + quality-gate resolution
substep.action: Run via Bash: `tmux-cli rules resolve --kind=convention` (append `--lang=<LANG> --framework=<FRAMEWORK>` when those signals are known, to override filesystem detection). It prints one convention rule-file path per line for THIS project's stack. Read EVERY printed file.

Run via Bash: `tmux-cli rules resolve --kind=code-rules` (same `--lang`/`--framework` flags when known). It prints the project's code-rule file paths. Read EVERY printed file — these define implementation/review conventions the later stages honor.

From the loaded convention packs, resolve the TOPOLOGY: SOURCE ROOTS (where production code lives), LAYER NAMES (the architectural layering the packs declare), ROOT NAMESPACE (the project's top-level namespace/module prefix), and the QUALITY-GATE COMMANDS (the static-analysis / lint / test commands the packs prescribe — emitted bare, host-style; the daemon wraps them at dispatch).

check.condition: `tmux-cli rules resolve` prints no convention files (empty catalogue / greenfield)
check.action: Apply greenfield DEFAULTS and mark each as a default (not a detected value): SOURCE ROOTS = ["src"]; LAYER NAMES = none declared; ROOT NAMESPACE = empty; QUALITY-GATE COMMANDS = none. Record that these are skeleton defaults to be confirmed at Stage 2, NOT detected project facts.

substep.rule: NEVER hardcode a source root, layer name, root namespace, quality-gate command, port, lang, or framework. Every such value is either resolved from the loaded packs/manifests above or is an explicitly-flagged greenfield default. No project name appears anywhere.

READ EVERY RESOLVED PACK IN FULL — reading a SUBSET is a DEFECT. Count the files each `tmux-cli rules resolve` prints (convention + code-rules) and READ ALL of them; skimming two and skipping the rest silently drops the rules/conventions those files carry — the exact cause of "missed the rules dimension" failures. The emitted CODE RULES pointers MUST list EVERY printed code-rule path (never a chosen subset); Stages 1/3/4 gate on the FULL set, and the quality-gate command list must reflect every gate the packs prescribe.

substep.title: Emit resolved capability + topology context block
substep.action: Compose ONE structured context block carrying everything the later stages consume:
substep.emit: • FEATURE: the parsed $ARGUMENTS description. • SIGNALS: lang, framework, run_target, has_database, has_frontend, frontend_mode, n_auth_flows — each with its source (detected / ASKed / greenfield-default) and rationale for any filled-in unknown. • E2E_RUNNER_AVAILABLE: true/false + source (config path / ASKed / default). • TOPOLOGY: source roots, layer names, root namespace, quality-gate commands — each tagged detected-from-packs vs greenfield-default. • CODE RULES: the resolved code-rule file paths (pointers for the implementation/validation stages to read), or "none". • SETTINGS: relevant .tmux-cli/setting.yaml values, or "absent".
substep.action: Hand this context block to Stage 1. Proceed to Stage 1.
